problem: The student mixed environment assumptions, failed early on a non-ASCII Windows path, tried a missing 7z dependency, and spent too long in generic PowerShell fallback loops before moving to high-signal reverse analysis.
rule_update: For Windows reverse cases, first normalize and verify the exact archive path, then use a fixed extraction fallback chain, and after extraction immediately classify the binary and escalate to targeted analysis. Do not keep broad string-dumping once the output becomes noisy or truncated.
checklist_update: Validate source archive path with literal-path-safe checks before extraction.
Prefer built-in Windows extraction methods before assuming external tools exist.
Record the extracted executable path before starting analysis.
After recovering prompts/imports, switch to PE triage or decompiler tooling.
Treat unverified string fragments as hints, not candidate flags.
